articles.title: Design history, as told through 125 objects
articles.summary: Cooper Hewitt’s new permanent collection exhibition is meant to be a conversation starter.

articles.title: Casa Luna / Nommo Arquitetos + Coti Arquitetura
articles.summary: At the end of a quiet street, nestled discreetly into the untouched Atlantic Forest landscape, Casa Luna is surrounded by araucaria trees and the songs of birds. Like a satellite, the house reflects the light around it and subtly contrasts with the nature that embraces it.

articles.title: PR House / 0studio Arquitectura
articles.summary: The project is organized around a central courtyard that articulates the different spaces and defines the living experience. This contained void not only regulates natural light and ventilation, but also establishes a continuous dialogue between interior and exterior, creating an atmosphere of calm and balance that accompanies every space.

articles.title: AMS House / Jacobsen Arquitetura
articles.summary: Carefully implemented on a sloping plot of land, the AMS House was built on the highest point of the lot, where the native forest serves as a backdrop and reference for the design of the house. Three blocks make up the architectural ensemble – with the social volume in the central position – connected by covered walkways. The strategy of volumetric fragmentation not only responds to the uneven terrain but also reinforces the intention of occupying the land.

articles.title: Larivière Foundation / Clusellas Ades + Machado-Silvetti
articles.summary: Choosing a lot on an industrial block in the La Boca neighborhood for the Larivière Foundation was the bold decision that originated this project, which benefited from the advisory of Facundo and Paula de Zuviría.

articles.title: Jacarezinho II House / Felipe Hess Arquitetos
articles.summary: Situated on a one-thousand-square-meter corner lot, the Jacarezinho House is born from the direct relationship between architecture and the garden. The strategy was to push the construction against one of the sides of the plot, freeing up about 60% of the area for a continuous garden, preserving existing trees, and structuring the project around this open space. The house occupies only 40% of the footprint, orienting all rooms toward this outdoor area.

articles.title: Dyes Inlet House / SHED Architecture & Design
articles.summary: This new primary home with a nearby guest house is designed with high-performance strategies to harmonize with its waterfront location on Washington's Kitsap Peninsula. The home reinterprets Scandinavian farmhouse forms to connect with its natural surroundings while providing a spacious and functional environment for an extended family. Designed for aging in place, the home focuses on comfort, energy efficiency and long-term family use.

articles.title: Viktoria House / Architekt Andreas Gruber
articles.summary: Viktoria's House is a concrete residence located in Vals, South Tyrol, at an elevation of approximately 1,350 meters above sea level. The house is set against steeply rising mountain slopes that transition from meadows and forests to rugged alpine peaks.

articles.title: Greenbuild International Conference + Expo 2026
articles.summary: Greenbuild hosts the largest annual event for green building professionals worldwide where attendees learn and source solutions to improve resilience, sustainability, and quality of life in our buildings, cities, and communities. Greenbuild will return to the east coast, taking place October 20-23, 2026, at the Javits Center in New York City.

articles.title: EDIFICA
articles.summary: Since its founding in 1990, Edifica has established itself as the most significant exhibition of its kind in Latin America. Organized biennially by the Chilean Chamber of Construction and Fisa, this major event serves as the primary meeting point for the entire industry value chain, bringing together leading suppliers, professional visitors, and representatives from both the public and private sectors.

articles.title: Rivers Before Roads: How Southeast Asia's Waterways Produced an Alternative Urbanism
articles.summary: For most of the twentieth century, architecture has learned to read cities through roads. Street hierarchies define urban plans, intersections organize movement, and buildings are understood by the façades they present to sidewalks. Roads appear so fundamental to urban life that they are often mistaken for a universal condition. Across much of Southeast Asia , cities developed according to an entirely different spatial logic. Long before automobiles reordered urban landscapes, rivers served as streets, marketplaces, civic spaces, and public infrastructure. Movement occurred primarily by boat,

articles.title: Restaurant Huset Svalbard / Snøhetta
articles.summary: The ever-changing light and contrasts of Svalbard inspired the new interior of the world's northernmost fine dining restaurant.

articles.title: Virtual Environments Are Changing the Way We Experience Architecture
articles.summary: There is a big difference between looking at an image of a building and experiencing the space it represents. The atmosphere of a place, shaped by its light, sounds, materiality, and relationship with the surrounding landscape, has long belonged to the realm of physical experience. Today, real-time rendering technologies are beginning to narrow that gap, allowing projects to be explored and experienced before they are ever built.

articles.title: "We Want to Learn Something New": In Conversation With the Curators of UIA World Congress of Architects Barcelona 2026
articles.summary: As Barcelona hosts the UIA World Congress of Architects for the second time in its history, thirty years after the 1996 edition, the city becomes a site for reflecting not only on architecture but also on the changing conditions under which it operates. Titled Becoming. Architectures for a Planet in Transition , and developed by the six-member curatorial team of Pau Bajet, Maria Giramé, Mariona Benedito, Tomeu Ramis, Pau Sarquella, and Carmen Torres, the 2026 Congress expands the discussion beyond the city toward planetary questions, addressing architecture through ecological, social, politica

articles.title: Remise Rosa Complex  / Hello Wood
articles.summary: Design & Build studio HELLO WOOD announces the completion of Remise Rosa, a 2,500 m² project that marks a definitive milestone in the studio's organic evolution. Transforming an abandoned railway site into a vibrant, three-level open-air oasis, Remise Rosa represents a sophisticated fusion of experimental design heritage and high-end permanent architecture.

articles.title: Teatro Mauri Restoration Preserves a 1951 Modernist Landmark in Valparaíso, Chile
articles.summary: In June 2026, the refurbished Teatro Mauri reopened its doors on Cerro Bellavista in Valparaíso, formerly Chile's main port . The building forms part of Latin America's modernist legacy and stands adjacent to La Sebastiana, one of the renowned residences of the poet Pablo Neruda. It was designed by architect Alfredo Vargas Stoller, author of other icons of modern architecture in Valparaíso, such as the Edificio Cooperativa Vitalicia and the Conjunto de Viviendas Vargas in Viña del Mar. Teatro Mauri opened in 1951 as a venue for performances and cinema. Following a fire in the early 1990s, it f

articles.title: Belonging to the Earth: Architecture in the Worldviews of Indigenous Peoples of Latin America
articles.summary: Anyone expecting the following words to discuss materiality, sustainable construction techniques, woodworking methods, or ways of weaving thatch will be mistaken. This article seeks precisely to shift the focus beyond the aspects that so often define discussions about Indigenous cultures when the subject is "architecture."

articles.title: 1X Technologies designs humanoid hand to "match or surpass human capability"
articles.summary: California-based company 1X Technologies has updated the hands of its humanoid robot Neo to "perform virtually any task a human can do with their hands". The hands will be installed on the first Neo robot model to be delivered to households, which are to ship this year, according to the company. The 5-foot-6-inch (167 cm) The post 1X Technologies designs humanoid hand to "match or surpass human capability" appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Ten stadiums of the future that rewrite the playbook
articles.summary: Stadiums are no longer just being designed for matchdays, with architects boldly pursuing novelty to create distinctive year-round landmarks. For our Future Stadium series, we look at 10 of the most offbeat examples currently in development. Manchester United, UK, by Foster + Partners This 100,000-seat stadium, designed for English football club Manchester United, dominated headlines The post Ten stadiums of the future that rewrite the playbook appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: File into the Future Stadium
articles.summary: With stadium construction intensifying and the World Cup drawing to a close, our Future Stadium series looks at the future of one of the world's most impactful buildings. We live in an age of entertainment, and it is no surprise that some of the most massive structures being built today are geared towards the delight The post File into the Future Stadium appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Will Gamble Architects adds "explicitly tropical" extension to Grade-II listed English cottage
articles.summary: A timber-framed glazed link and courtyard garden connect old and new parts of this home in Northwood, UK, which architecture studio Will Gamble Architects designed to reference traditional Sri Lankan houses. Named Muttram Veedu after the Tamil term for a courtyard house, Will Gamble Architects completed the extension and refurbishment for clients with Sri Lankan The post Will Gamble Architects adds "explicitly tropical" extension to Grade-II listed English cottage appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Barrel-vaulted community greenhouse built at the Barbican
articles.summary: Architecture firms Studio Folk Architects and Raskl have built a timber-framed greenhouse, named The Veggery, at the Barbican Estate as part of the London Festival of Architecture. Hexagonal in shape, The Veggery was topped with a multi-arched polytunnel roof that references the barrel-vaulted roofs of the Barbican. Set to be in place next to the The post Barrel-vaulted community greenhouse built at the Barbican appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: YSG draws on art deco for Sydney apartment revamp
articles.summary: Chamfered surfaces, warm hues and reinstated art deco features define the interiors of The Gentleman, a Sydney apartment remodelled by local interiors studio YSG. YSG carried out a complete renovation of the 95-square-meter, high-ceilinged apartment in a 1929 art deco building in the trendy suburb of Redfern, which has views of the Sydney Opera House. The post YSG draws on art deco for Sydney apartment revamp appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: Mesh-89 device lets Palestinian drivers stay ahead of checkpoint closures
articles.summary: Recent Central Saint Martins graduate Joud Abuhomos has invented a simple, off-grid communication device called Mesh-89, designed to alert Palestinian bus drivers to the status of the West Bank's 89 permanent checkpoints. Deliberately unassuming in appearance, Mesh-89 is intended to help drivers navigate the everyday problem of traffic restriction around Israeli-run checkpoints, which can cause The post Mesh-89 device lets Palestinian drivers stay ahead of checkpoint closures appeared first on Dezeen .

articles.title: A Candle-Powered Sculptural Light
articles.summary: Prior to electricity, lighthouses used open flame and lenses to project light. This concept fascinated Parisian design firm Studio Douze Degrés , who subsequently created this Flare object. Aluminum-bodied and candle-powered, it's equal parts lamp and sculpture: These run €390 (USD $445).

articles.title: How to Unlock and Start Your Car When the Key Fob Battery is Dead
articles.summary: The coin batteries in automotive key fobs only last for a few years. But if they happen to die and you're caught unawares, auto manufacturers have designed these little-known backup methods to enter and start your vehicle. To the right of your driver's door handle, you'll see this cap. On the underside is a notch. You can stick the blade of the key into this notch to pry the cap off. This reveals a conventional lock cylinder. If your fob does not have the switchblade key, it likely has a hidden conventional key sheathed within the fob. Look for parting lines to work out where it is. As for sta

articles.title: Rethinking Cheapie Inline Lamp Switches
articles.summary: The inline switch common on cheap lamps is often an unloved, poorly-integrated plastic lump. Vancouver-based industrial designer Andrew King suggests a fix, with his self-directed Modal Inline Switch project: Modal rethinks a component that's usually treated as an afterthought. Most are plastic, generic, and visually disconnected from the objects they control. This project treats the switch as something worth designing. The housing is CNC-machined from aluminum, with a low, rounded form that sits naturally along the cord and stays put on a surface. A latching push-button sits flush within the

articles.summary: Ever wondered how gearboxes and torque work? MetMo’s latest EDC turns the physics into fidget fun There is a piece by kinetic artist Arthur Ganson called Beholding the Big Bang, a gear train stretching over a meter long, where the final...

articles.title: This elastic band-powered car proves you don’t need an engine to experience real mechanical thrills
articles.summary: This elastic band-powered car proves you don’t need an engine to experience real mechanical thrills Kinetic energy is a fascinating thing, a fact I first realized after becoming obsessed with pull-back toy cars as a child. The simple act of...

articles.title: This Lithuanian Forest Retreat Glows Like a Lantern at the Edge of the Trees
articles.summary: This Lithuanian Forest Retreat Glows Like a Lantern at the Edge of the Trees There’s a moment, just after dusk, when the Sanctum nature retreat reveals its most compelling quality. The gabled timber structures, dark and quiet against the...

articles.title: How A Simple Angle Makes Outdoor Seating Feel More Social
articles.summary: Simple geometric adjustments to outdoor benches and tables can subtly influence how passersby interact with space. By reconsidering angles, designers encourage pause and conversation in public areas. This approach showcases the power of minor tweaks in urban furniture design.

articles.title: Galaxy Z Fold 8: July 22 date confirmed, S Pen returns
articles.summary: Samsung confirms the Galaxy Z Fold 8 will launch on July 22, reintroducing the S Pen. The device continues the folding phone trend with a slimmer chassis and updated hardware. STO highlights the brand’s ongoing commitment to foldable smartphone refinement.

articles.title: A LEGO Fan Recreated Gustav Klimt’s Iconic Artwork ‘The Kiss,’ Gold Studs and All
articles.summary: A LEGO enthusiast painstakingly reconstructed Klimt’s “The Kiss” using gold studs for detail. The project merges pop culture building with art history, paying homage to early 20th-century aesthetics. It demonstrates the potential for creative reinterpretation across media.

articles.title: Lenovo Fit a 16GB Intel Lunar Lake PC Into the Size of a Coffee Table Book
articles.summary: Lenovo's ThinkCentre Neo 50q Gen 6 is disguised as a faux hardcover book for a sleek, compact PC experience. The device packs 16GB of Intel Lunar Lake performance in a hand‑friendly form factor. This showcases Lenovo's innovation in blending hardware with designRW.

articles.title: Honor Watch 6 Review: Great Endurance, Not Quite Perfect
articles.summary: Honor’s Watch 6 introduces a round face and a visual reset, followed by solid battery life. The design shift aims to attract users from the square‑faced Watch 5 base. However, some user experience issues remain evident in real‑world testing.

articles.title: You can finally own the upside down driving Spéirling Pure electric hypercar
articles.summary: McMurtry Automotive brings its Spéirling Pure electric hypercar prototype to market, notable for its upside‑down racing capability. The vehicle emphasizes aerodynamics and extreme traction, promising record‑breaking performance. This marks a milestone for electric hypercar viability.

articles.title: Anoma’s A1 Prehistoric Is Proof That Scars Can Be Art
articles.summary: Anoma A1 Prehistoric watch merges artistic motifs with tangible wear marks, challenging conventional watch aesthetics. Thesertation focuses on scars as deliberate design elements, exploring personal narrative through timekeeping. It pushes the frontier of expressive wearables.

articles.title: To the Red Dot Newsroom
articles.summary: Red Dot Design firm opens a new newsroom space to communicate design ethos. The layout prioritizes collaborative interaction with visitors and industry partners. It represents a strategic move to strengthen brand engagement.

articles.title: General press information on the Red Dot Design Award
articles.summary: Red Dot Design Award provides comprehensive press resources for media coverage. Information includes event schedules, award Daly categories, and media kits. The initiative aims to streamline industry outreach.

articles.title: Explore a Modern Michigan Home Designed Around a Rare Porsche Collection
articles.summary: Khanna Schultz re Graf the suburban home into pavilions, featuring a hidden bourbon room to house a Porsche collection. The architecture blends functional living spaces with curated automotive exhibits. It presents a new benchmark for niche collection homes.

articles.title: SURI Reimagines Toothpaste With a Refillable, Displayable System
articles.summary: SURI introduces Restore Gel with a reusable dispenser and compostable refills, emphasizing eco‑friendly packaging. The system supports science‑backed toothpaste formula and encourages minimal waste. This product aligns sustainability with daily dental habits.

articles.title: Amra Tareen and the Future of AI Visualization For Designers
articles.summary: Amra Tareen of ALL3D.AI offers AI‑driven spatial visualization tools for design professionals. The platform automates 3D development and integrates client collaboration features. It represents a shift toward AI‑enabled design workflows.

articles.title: Hovis and Kingsmill are now one business – and BrandOpus has given it an identity with no wheat in sight
articles.summary: BrandOpus crafted a refreshed identity for the merged Hovis Bakeries, avoiding wheat imagery to reduce cultural connotations. The design focuses on heritage, simplicity, and modern branding language. It demonstrates strategic rebranding in the food sector.

articles.title: How Houth Studio turns Taiwanese culture into bold graphic design
articles.summary: Houth Studio applies bold typography and vivid graphics to convey Taiwanese heritage strategically. The studio merges cultural narratives with contemporary visual language, creating visually compelling branding. Their portfolio showcases storytelling through design.

articles.title: What if clothing could tell the whole story? Hannah Knox on painting 'portraits without people'
articles.summary: Hannah Knox transforms garments into painted studies that narrate identity and desire. Her work diverges from traditional fashion photography, offering tactile storytelling. The pieces reflect broader conversations on material culture and memory.

articles.title: Can stories save us? JAIKU is putting 60 people in a room to find out
articles.summary:
The JAIKU Storytelling Summit lands in London this September, bringing founders, marketers and creative leaders together to ask whether narrative still has the power to change things.

In a media l...

articles.title: carved from solid carrara marble, abstract house-like objects explore architectural form
articles.summary: An artist carves abstract marble forms that echo architectural silhouettes. The pieces integrate into model intervention spaces, provoking spatial thinking. The work sits at the intersection of sculpture and architecture.

articles.title: david chipperfield revives historic waterfront landmark as new museum in spain
articles.summary: David Chipperfield Architects rejuvenates the Pereda Building into a contemporary art, culture, and tech museum in Santander. The adaptive reuse preserves historic elements while integrating modern galleries and performance spaces. This landmark project highlights the revival of civic architecture.

articles.title: modernist-inspired café framed by layered wood and steel extends almaty museum of arts
articles.summary: The new café adjoining-maker 'almaty museum of arts uses layered wood and steel to echo Soviet modernist principles. The structure maintains geometric clarity and open sightlines that complement the museum's aesthetic. The extension serves both functional hospitality and architectural coherence.

articles.title: what we learned: craftingوبه the future
articles.summary: Designboom reflects on design practice, emphasizing craft, collaboration, and long‑term stewardship. The article outlines lessons gained from recent projects and design trends. It advocates a shift from speed to attentive, sustainable creation.

articles.title: century-old japanese cedar werenfolds into flat-pack furniture collection, FLAT/FORM
articles.summary: A new flat‑pack furniture line uses ancient Japanese cedar, extending the wood’s life cycle. The collection offers modular units enabling easy assembly and disassembly, supporting reuse. It showcases a sustainable approach to high‑quality woodworking.

articles.title: recycled cables transform this pair of adidas sneakers into a one-of-one design
articles.summary: Artist Cory Infinite repurposes recycled cables into bespoke Adidas Adistar XLG 2.0 sneakers for Teezo Touchdown. The unique design transforms waste into a wearable statement piece. This collaboration highlights circular fashion trends.

articles.title: an archipelago of botanical sculptures by agostino iacur записi fills historic courtyard in milan
articles.summary: Plant-inspired steel sculptures by Agostino Iacurci populate a Milan historic courtyard, echoing 1895 illustrations. The installation blends art and botanical themes, turning a civic space into a green gallery. The work showcases contemporary sculpture’s relevance to urban heritage.

articles.title: interconnected metal rods form a luminous winged installation on china’s coastline
articles.summary: An installation of copper plates and acrylic lightboxes creates a luminous wing with interlocking metal rods along China's coast. The piece blends入力 art with natural landscape, featuring immersive lighting effects. It demonstrates modern public art’s power in coastal tourism.

articles.title: Airwheel Launches AI Cabin Suitcase, a Luxury Electric Rideable 20inch Carry on Hand Suitcase for Next-Generation Smart Travel Suitcase
articles.summary: Airwheel introduces an AI-driven, electric suitcase capable of auto‑driving and rapid charging. The 20‑inch hand‑suitcase models provide smart travel convenience. It targets high‑end, tech‑savvy globetrotters.

articles.title: A' Security, Safety and Surveillance Products Design Awards 2026 Call for Nominations Announced
articles.summary: A' Security, Safety and Surveillance Products Design Awards opens calls for 2026 nominations across security and safety sectors. The awards recognize achievements in product design innovation. The announcement invites professionals to showcase their solutions.

articles.title: 7 Best Gadgets & Gear for Digital Nomads in 2026 That Turn Any Café Into a Proper Studio
articles.summary: Yanko Design lists seven gadgets ideal for digital nomads, enabling conversion of cafés into mobile studios. Items range from portable Wi‑Fi rigs to collapsible ergonomic chairs. The guide supports remote working lifestyles.

articles.title: iPhone 18 Pro Max may trade slimmer design for bigger battery
articles.summary: Rumors suggest Apple’s iPhone 18 Pro Max will feature a thicker form factor to house a larger battery. The trade‑off prioritizes endurance over the slim aesthetic of contemporary iPhones. The speculation indicates Apple’s shift toward longevity.

articles.title: Sparkhood by Jizheng Guan Wins Iron in A' Digital Product Design Award
articles.summary: Sparkhood, by Jizheng Guan, secures an Iron award in the 2026 A' Digital Product Design Contest. The product’s innovative stove‑for‑home concept was lauded for its minimalist interface. This accolade spotlights Asian designers’ influence on global design.

articles.title: Submit Now To Interior Design’s 2026 Best Of Year Awards
articles.summary: Interior Design Magazine invites entries for its annual Best Of Year Awards, recognizing exemplary interior design work. The submission window encourages professionals to demonstrate excellence in architecture and design. The call promotes industry standards and showcases standout projects.

articles.title: Samsung Wins Two Red Dot Award: Design Concept ‘Best of the Best’ Awards
articles.summary: Samsung secured two prestigious Red Dot awards for its design concepts, highlighting innovation and excellence. The company showcased a range of future-oriented products that received top honors. This success underscores Samsung’s strong position in global product design and technology.

articles.title: Hankook, Bridgestone, Kumho earn Red Dot awards for tire design
articles.summary: Three major tire manufacturers—Hankook, Bridgestone, and Kumho—were each awarded a Red Dot for their cutting‑edge tire designs. The awards recognize advanced performance, durability, and aesthetic appeal in automotive tire engineering. The recognition reinforces their competitive edge in the global tire market.

articles.title: Samsung Sweeps Red Dot Design Awards With AI Concepts
articles.summary: Samsung dominated the Red Dot在线电影 by winning multiple awards for AI‑Да-centric product concepts. The accolades cover a diverse range of consumer electronic items, all featuring advanced artificial intelligence. The sweep reinforces Samsung’s leadership in AI‑driven design innovation.

articles.title: Samsung Electronics Wins Top Honors at 'Red Dot Design Award 2026'... Takes Home 8 Awards
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics celebrated a record eight Red Dot Design Awards in 2026, covering a broad spectrum of product categories. The awards highlight Samsung’s excellence in design, usability, and technology integration. This achievement further cements the brand’s reputation as a global design leader.

articles.title: Samsung Wins Top Prize at Red Dot Design Awards 2026
articles.summary: Samsung clinched the top prize at the 2026 Red Dot Design Awards, showcasing standout product design. The award was part of a larger set of recognitions for the company’s design innovations. The accolade highlights Samsung’s ongoing commitment to superior design standards worldwide.

articles.title: Samsung Electronics’ Kids Robot Wins Top Prize at Red Dot Design Award 2026
articles.summary: Samsung’s children’s robot project captured the top prize at the 2026 Red Dot Design Awards. The award recognizes the robot’s intuitive design, child‑friendly interface, and advanced robotics features. This win showcases Samsung’s expansion into educational and lifestyle robotics.

articles.title: Hankook Tire Wins Gold at A' Design Award for Upcycled Products
articles.summary: Hankook Tire received a gold award at the A' Design competition for its upcycled tire products. The project focuses on sustainability and creative reuse, turning used materials into functional tire components. The award emphasizes Hankook’s leading stance in eco‑friendly design.

articles.title: Philips TV & Philips Sound celebrate 2026 Red Dot Design Award success
articles.summary: Philips’ TV and sound system lines were recognized at the 2026 Red Dot Design Awards for their aesthetic and functional excellence. Both product lines received accolades highlighting design quality and user experience. The awards bolster Philips’ reputation for high‑end design in consumer electronics.

articles.title: Cardo Wins Prestigious Red Dot Award for Innovative CardoBeyond GTS Helmet
articles.summary: Cardo was awarded a Red Dot for its innovative CardoBeyond GTS helmet, which integrates advanced safety and connectivity technologies. The design award highlights the helmet’s superior ergonomic features and tech‑driven functionality. The win underscores Cardo’s position as an innovator Welcome toernos.

articles.title: IOE Thapathali student wins Asian Paints Kaleidoscope 2025-26 design competition
articles.summary: A student from IOE Thapathali won the Asian Paints Kaleidoscope design competition for 2025-26. The project showcased a creative approach to paint-based design themes, earning recognition for originality and execution. The win encourages student participation in regional design contests.
